summary: Round-1 fixes are in place: cross-origin HTTP/WS requests are rejected, navigation is scheme-confined, duplicate normalized step ids are rejected, and the ephemeral temp profile is removed on shutdown. One new material defect remains in the loopback-Origin allowlist.

findings.title: IPv6 loopback same-origin requests are incorrectly rejected by the new Origin guard
findings.recommendation: `new URL(origin).hostname` returns `[::1]`, not `::1`, so the current equality check rejects legitimate same-origin requests when the UI is served from `http://[::1]:...`. Normalize IPv6 hostnames before comparison or explicitly allow `[::1]` in both the HTTP and WS upgrade guards.
